1:import { app } from '@azure/functions';
2:import type { HttpRequest, HttpResponseInit, InvocationContext } from '@azure/functions';
3:import { verifyFirebaseIdToken } from '../../services/firebaseAdmin.js';
4:import { bookingRepo } from '../../cosmos/booking-repository.js';
5:import {
6:  createComplaint,
7:  findActiveComplaintByBookingAndParty,
8:} from '../../cosmos/complaints-repository.js';
9:import { sendOwnerComplaintFiled } from '../../services/fcm.service.js';
10:import {
11:  CreateComplaintByPartnerBodySchema,
12:  CustomerReasonCodeEnum,
13:  TechnicianReasonCodeEnum,
14:} from '../../schemas/complaint.js';
15:import type { ComplaintDoc, PartnerComplaintResponse } from '../../schemas/complaint.js';
16:import { randomUUID } from 'crypto';
18:export async function partnerCreateComplaintHandler(
19:  req: HttpRequest,
20:  ctx: InvocationContext,
21:): Promise<HttpResponseInit> {
22:  const auth = req.headers.get('authorization') ?? '';
23:  if (!auth.startsWith('Bearer ')) return { status: 401, jsonBody: { code: 'UNAUTHENTICATED' } };
25:  let uid: string;
26:  try {
27:    const decoded = await verifyFirebaseIdToken(auth.slice(7));
28:    uid = decoded.uid;
29:  } catch {
30:    return { status: 401, jsonBody: { code: 'TOKEN_INVALID' } };
31:  }
33:  let body: unknown;
34:  try { body = await req.json(); } catch {
35:    return { status: 400, jsonBody: { code: 'INVALID_JSON' } };
36:  }
37:  const parsed = CreateComplaintByPartnerBodySchema.safeParse(body);
38:  if (!parsed.success) {
39:    return { status: 400, jsonBody: { code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', issues: parsed.error.issues } };
40:  }
41:  const data = parsed.data;
43:  const booking = await bookingRepo.getById(data.bookingId);
44:  if (!booking) return { status: 404, jsonBody: { code: 'BOOKING_NOT_FOUND' } };
46:  const isCustomer = booking.customerId === uid;
47:  const isTechnician = booking.technicianId === uid;
48:  if (!isCustomer && !isTechnician) return { status: 403, jsonBody: { code: 'FORBIDDEN' } };
49:  if (booking.status !== 'CLOSED') {
50:    return { status: 409, jsonBody: { code: 'BOOKING_NOT_ELIGIBLE', status: booking.status } };
51:  }
53:  const filedBy = isCustomer ? 'CUSTOMER' as const : 'TECHNICIAN' as const;
54:  const reasonValid = filedBy === 'CUSTOMER'
55:    ? CustomerReasonCodeEnum.safeParse(data.reasonCode).success
56:    : TechnicianReasonCodeEnum.safeParse(data.reasonCode).success;
57:  if (!reasonValid) return { status: 400, jsonBody: { code: 'INVALID_REASON_CODE' } };
59:  const existing = await findActiveComplaintByBookingAndParty(data.bookingId, uid, filedBy);
60:  if (existing) return { status: 409, jsonBody: { code: 'COMPLAINT_ALREADY_FILED' } };
62:  const now = new Date();
63:  const doc: ComplaintDoc = {
64:    id: randomUUID(),
65:    orderId: data.bookingId,
66:    customerId: booking.customerId,
67:    technicianId: booking.technicianId ?? '',
68:    description: data.description,
69:    status: 'NEW',
70:    internalNotes: [],
71:    slaDeadlineAt: new Date(now.getTime() + 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString(),
72:    acknowledgeDeadlineAt: new Date(now.getTime() + 2 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString(),
73:    escalated: false,
74:    ackBreached: false,
75:    createdAt: now.toISOString(),
76:    updatedAt: now.toISOString(),
77:    filedBy,
78:    reasonCode: data.reasonCode,
79:    ...(data.photoStoragePath ? { photoStoragePath: data.photoStoragePath } : {}),
80:  };
82:  await createComplaint(doc);
84:  sendOwnerComplaintFiled({ bookingId: data.bookingId, filedBy, reasonCode: data.reasonCode })
85:    .catch((err: unknown) => ctx.error('sendOwnerComplaintFiled failed', err));
87:  const response: PartnerComplaintResponse = {
88:    id: doc.id,
89:    status: doc.status,
90:    filedBy: doc.filedBy,
91:    reasonCode: doc.reasonCode,
92:    acknowledgeDeadlineAt: doc.acknowledgeDeadlineAt,
93:    slaDeadlineAt: doc.slaDeadlineAt,
94:    createdAt: doc.createdAt,
95:    updatedAt: doc.updatedAt,
96:  };
97:  return { status: 201, jsonBody: response };
98:}
100:app.http('partnerCreateComplaint', {
101:  methods: ['POST'],
102:  route: 'v1/complaints',
103:  authLevel: 'anonymous',
104:  handler: partnerCreateComplaintHandler,
105:});

1:package com.homeservices.technician.navigation
3:import android.content.Intent
4:import android.net.Uri
5:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Box
6:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize
7:import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
8:import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
9:import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
10:import androidx.hilt.navigation.compose.hiltViewModel
11:import androidx.navigation.NavController
12:import androidx.navigation.NavGraphBuilder
13:import androidx.navigation.NavType
14:import androidx.navigation.compose.composable
15:import androidx.navigation.compose.navigation
16:import androidx.navigation.navArgument
17:import com.homeservices.technician.domain.activeJob.model.NavigationEvent
18:import com.homeservices.technician.ui.activeJob.ActiveJobScreen
19:import com.homeservices.technician.ui.activeJob.ActiveJobViewModel
20:import com.homeservices.technician.ui.complaint.ComplaintRoutes
21:import com.homeservices.technician.ui.complaint.ComplaintScreen
22:import com.homeservices.technician.ui.rating.RatingRoutes
23:import com.homeservices.technician.ui.rating.RatingScreen
25:internal fun NavGraphBuilder.homeGraph(navController: NavController) {
26:    navigation(startDestination = "home_dashboard", route = "home") {
27:        composable("home_dashboard") {
28:            Box(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize())
29:        }
30:        composable(
31:            route = "activeJob/{bookingId}",
32:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("bookingId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
33:        ) {
34:            val viewModel: ActiveJobViewModel = hiltViewModel()
35:            val context = LocalContext.current
36:            LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
37:                viewModel.navigationEvents.collect { event ->
38:                    if (event is NavigationEvent.Maps) {
39:                        context.startActivity(
40:                            Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, Uri.parse(event.uri)).apply {
41:                                addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
42:                            },
43:                        )
44:                    }
45:                }
46:            }
47:            ActiveJobScreen(viewModel = viewModel)
48:        }
49:        composable(
50:            route = RatingRoutes.ROUTE,
51:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("bookingId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
52:        ) {
53:            RatingScreen()
54:        }
55:        composable(
56:            route = ComplaintRoutes.ROUTE,
57:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("bookingId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
58:        ) { backStackEntry ->
59:            val bookingId = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("bookingId") ?: ""
60:            ComplaintScreen(bookingId = bookingId, onBack = { navController.popBackStack() })
61:        }
62:    }
63:}

1:package com.homeservices.customer.navigation
3:import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
4:import androidx.hilt.navigation.compose.hiltViewModel
5:import androidx.navigation.NavController
6:import androidx.navigation.NavGraphBuilder
7:import androidx.navigation.NavType
8:import androidx.navigation.compose.composable
9:import androidx.navigation.navArgument
10:import androidx.navigation.navigation
11:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.booking.AddressScreen
12:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.booking.BookingConfirmedScreen
13:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.booking.BookingSummaryScreen
14:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.booking.BookingViewModel
15:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.booking.PriceApprovalScreen
16:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.booking.PriceApprovalViewModel
17:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.booking.SlotPickerScreen
18:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.catalogue.CatalogueHomeScreen
19:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.catalogue.CatalogueHomeViewModel
20:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.catalogue.ServiceDetailScreen
21:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.catalogue.ServiceDetailViewModel
22:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.catalogue.ServiceListScreen
23:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.catalogue.ServiceListViewModel
24:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.complaint.ComplaintRoutes
25:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.complaint.ComplaintScreen
26:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.rating.RatingRoutes
27:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.rating.RatingScreen
28:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.tracking.LiveTrackingScreen
29:import com.homeservices.customer.ui.tracking.LiveTrackingViewModel
31:internal fun NavGraphBuilder.mainGraph(navController: NavController) {
32:    navigation(startDestination = CatalogueRoutes.HOME, route = "main") {
33:        composable(CatalogueRoutes.HOME) {
34:            val vm: CatalogueHomeViewModel = hiltViewModel()
35:            CatalogueHomeScreen(
36:                viewModel = vm,
37:                onCategoryClick = { id -> navController.navigate(CatalogueRoutes.serviceList(id)) },
38:            )
39:        }
40:        composable(
41:            route = CatalogueRoutes.SERVICE_LIST,
42:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("categoryId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
43:        ) {
44:            val vm: ServiceListViewModel = hiltViewModel()
45:            ServiceListScreen(
46:                viewModel = vm,
47:                onServiceClick = { id -> navController.navigate(CatalogueRoutes.serviceDetail(id)) },
48:                onBack = { navController.popBackStack() },
49:            )
50:        }
51:        composable(
52:            route = CatalogueRoutes.SERVICE_DETAIL,
53:            arguments =
54:                listOf(
55:                    navArgument("serviceId") { type = NavType.StringType },
56:                    navArgument("techId") {
57:                        type = NavType.StringType
58:                        nullable = true
59:                        defaultValue = null
60:                    },
61:                ),
62:        ) {
63:            val vm: ServiceDetailViewModel = hiltViewModel()
64:            ServiceDetailScreen(
65:                viewModel = vm,
66:                onBookNow = { svcId, catId ->
67:                    navController.navigate(BookingRoutes.slotPicker(svcId, catId))
68:                },
69:                onBack = { navController.popBackStack() },
70:            )
71:        }
72:    }
74:    // Booking flow — BookingViewModel scoped to the booking nested graph
75:    navigation(
76:        startDestination = BookingRoutes.SLOT_PICKER,
77:        route = BookingRoutes.BOOKING_GRAPH,
78:    ) {
79:        composable(
80:            route = BookingRoutes.SLOT_PICKER,
81:            arguments =
82:                listOf(
83:                    navArgument("serviceId") { type = NavType.StringType },
84:                    navArgument("categoryId") { type = NavType.StringType },
85:                ),
86:        ) { backStackEntry ->
87:            val bookingEntry =
88:                remember(backStackEntry) {
89:                    navController.getBackStackEntry(BookingRoutes.BOOKING_GRAPH)
90:                }
91:            val vm: BookingViewModel = hiltViewModel(bookingEntry)
92:            val serviceId = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("serviceId") ?: ""
93:            val categoryId = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("categoryId") ?: ""
94:            SlotPickerScreen(
95:                onSlotSelected = { slot ->
96:                    vm.pendingServiceId = serviceId
97:                    vm.pendingCategoryId = categoryId
98:                    vm.setSlotAndAddress(slot, "", 0.0, 0.0)
99:                    navController.navigate(BookingRoutes.ADDRESS)
100:                },
101:                onBack = { navController.popBackStack() },
102:            )
103:        }
105:        composable(BookingRoutes.ADDRESS) { backStackEntry ->
106:            val bookingEntry =
107:                remember(backStackEntry) {
108:                    navController.getBackStackEntry(BookingRoutes.BOOKING_GRAPH)
109:                }
110:            val vm: BookingViewModel = hiltViewModel(bookingEntry)
111:            AddressScreen(
112:                onAddressConfirmed = { addressText, lat, lng ->
113:                    val state = vm.uiState.value
114:                    val slot =
115:                        (state as? com.homeservices.customer.ui.booking.BookingUiState.Ready)?.slot
116:                            ?: return@AddressScreen
117:                    vm.setSlotAndAddress(slot, addressText, lat, lng)
118:                    navController.navigate(BookingRoutes.SUMMARY)
119:                },
120:                onBack = { navController.popBackStack() },
121:            )
122:        }
124:        composable(BookingRoutes.SUMMARY) { backStackEntry ->
125:            val bookingEntry =
126:                remember(backStackEntry) {
127:                    navController.getBackStackEntry(BookingRoutes.BOOKING_GRAPH)
128:                }
129:            val vm: BookingViewModel = hiltViewModel(bookingEntry)
130:            BookingSummaryScreen(
131:                viewModel = vm,
132:                serviceId = vm.pendingServiceId,
133:                categoryId = vm.pendingCategoryId,
134:                onConfirmed = { bookingId ->
135:                    navController.navigate(BookingRoutes.confirmedRoute(bookingId)) {
136:                        popUpTo(BookingRoutes.BOOKING_GRAPH) { inclusive = true }
137:                    }
138:                },
139:                onBack = { navController.popBackStack() },
140:            )
141:        }
143:        composable(
144:            route = BookingRoutes.CONFIRMED,
145:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("bookingId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
146:        ) { backStackEntry ->
147:            val bookingId = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("bookingId") ?: ""
148:            BookingConfirmedScreen(
149:                bookingId = bookingId,
150:                onBackToHome = {
151:                    navController.popBackStack(CatalogueRoutes.HOME, inclusive = false)
152:                },
153:                onTrackBooking = { id ->
154:                    navController.navigate(BookingRoutes.liveTrackingRoute(id))
155:                },
156:            )
157:        }
159:        composable(
160:            route = BookingRoutes.PRICE_APPROVAL,
161:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("bookingId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
162:        ) { backStackEntry ->
163:            val vm: PriceApprovalViewModel = hiltViewModel()
164:            PriceApprovalScreen(
165:                viewModel = vm,
166:                bookingId = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("bookingId") ?: "",
167:                onApprovalComplete = { navController.popBackStack() },
168:            )
169:        }
171:        composable(
172:            route = BookingRoutes.LIVE_TRACKING,
173:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("bookingId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
174:        ) {
175:            val vm: LiveTrackingViewModel = hiltViewModel()
176:            LiveTrackingScreen(
177:                viewModel = vm,
178:                onBack = { navController.popBackStack() },
179:            )
180:        }
182:        composable(
183:            route = RatingRoutes.ROUTE,
184:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("bookingId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
185:        ) {
186:            RatingScreen()
187:        }
189:        composable(
190:            route = ComplaintRoutes.ROUTE,
191:            arguments = listOf(navArgument("bookingId") { type = NavType.StringType }),
192:        ) { backStackEntry ->
193:            val bookingId = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("bookingId") ?: ""
194:            ComplaintScreen(
195:                bookingId = bookingId,
196:                onBack = { navController.popBackStack() },
197:            )
198:        }
199:    }
200:}

docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1750-round2.md:23669:- [ ] **T2 â€” API: `POST /v1/complaints` endpoint (api/, TDD)**
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1750-round2.md:23671:  - [ ] Create `api/src/functions/complaints/partner-create.ts` â€” route `POST /v1/complaints`; auth: raw `verifyFirebaseIdToken` (handles both customer and technician tokens); fetch booking from Cosmos to derive `filedBy`; validate `reasonCode` against per-role enums; build `ComplaintDoc` with `filedBy`, `reasonCode`, `photoStoragePath`, `acknowledgeDeadlineAt = now+2h`, `slaDeadlineAt = now+24h`, `type = "STANDARD"`; duplicate-check query against complaints container; call `createComplaint()`; fire FCM `OWNER_COMPLAINT_FILED` to `owner_alerts` fire-and-forget; return 201
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1750-round2.md:23674:- [ ] **T3 â€” API: `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}` endpoint (api/, TDD)**
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1750-round2.md:23676:  - [ ] Create `api/src/functions/complaints/partner-get.ts` â€” route `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}`; same dual-role auth; return complaints for that booking filed by caller (`filedBy` + `uid` filter on query)

docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1750-round2.md:23750:`POST /v1/complaints` cannot use `requireCustomer` HOF because it must accept both customer and technician tokens. The server sets `filedBy` â€” the request body never includes it (prevents forgery).

docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23744:39:- **Then** `POST /v1/complaints` is called with `{ bookingId, reasonCode, description, photoStoragePath? }`
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23747:46:- **When** `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}` is called by the same party who filed it
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23764:89:- [ ] **T2 — API: `POST /v1/complaints` endpoint (api/, TDD)**
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23766:91:  - [ ] Create `api/src/functions/complaints/partner-create.ts` — route `POST /v1/complaints`; auth: raw `verifyFirebaseIdToken` (handles both customer and technician tokens); fetch booking from Cosmos to derive `filedBy`; validate `reasonCode` against per-role enums; build `ComplaintDoc` with `filedBy`, `reasonCode`, `photoStoragePath`, `acknowledgeDeadlineAt = now+2h`, `slaDeadlineAt = now+24h`, `type = "STANDARD"`; duplicate-check query against complaints container; call `createComplaint()`; fire FCM `OWNER_COMPLAINT_FILED` to `owner_alerts` fire-and-forget; return 201
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23768:94:- [ ] **T3 — API: `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}` endpoint (api/, TDD)**
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23770:96:  - [ ] Create `api/src/functions/complaints/partner-get.ts` — route `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}`; same dual-role auth; return complaints for that booking filed by caller (`filedBy` + `uid` filter on query)
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23775:107:  - [ ] Create `customer-app/.../data/complaint/remote/dto/ComplaintDtos.kt` — Moshi `@JsonClass(generateAdapter=true)` data classes: `CreateComplaintRequestDto`, `ComplaintResponseDto`, `ComplaintListResponseDto`
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23797:170:`POST /v1/complaints` cannot use `requireCustomer` HOF because it must accept both customer and technician tokens. Pattern: call `verifyFirebaseIdToken(token)` directly, fetch the booking doc, check `uid == booking.customerId` → `filedBy = CUSTOMER` or `uid == booking.technicianId` → `filedBy = TECHNICIAN`; else 403. The server sets `filedBy` — the request body never includes it (prevents forgery).
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23801:181:### `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}` — filedBy filter

docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23901:import com.homeservices.technician.data.complaint.remote.dto.ComplaintResponseDto
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:23910:        public operator fun invoke(bookingId: String): Flow<Result<List<ComplaintResponseDto>>> = repo.getComplaintsForBooking(bookingId)
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:24333:7:export async function partnerGetComplaintsHandler(
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:24362:36:app.http('partnerGetComplaints', {
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:24366:40:  handler: partnerGetComplaintsHandler,
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:24375:  `partnerCreateComplaintHandler` currently returns the full `ComplaintDoc`. That exposes both parties' Firebase UIDs (`customerId`/`technicianId`) and other internal fields to whichever side filed the complaint. Because this is a partner-facing endpoint, the response should be reduced to the public status fields the apps actually need instead of echoing the stored document verbatim.
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:24381:  `POST /v1/complaints` now rejects any booking whose status is not `CLOSED`, but this button is shown on the screen displayed right after a booking is placed. In practice, every customer who taps it from here gets a guaranteed `409 BOOKING_NOT_ELIGIBLE`, so the new entry point is wired to a state where the backend can never accept it.
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:24393:  `partnerCreateComplaintHandler` currently returns the full `ComplaintDoc`. That exposes both parties' Firebase UIDs (`customerId`/`technicianId`) and other internal fields to whichever side filed the complaint. Because this is a partner-facing endpoint, the response should be reduced to the public status fields the apps actually need instead of echoing the stored document verbatim.
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1836-round5.md:24399:  `POST /v1/complaints` now rejects any booking whose status is not `CLOSED`, but this button is shown on the screen displayed right after a booking is placed. In practice, every customer who taps it from here gets a guaranteed `409 BOOKING_NOT_ELIGIBLE`, so the new entry point is wired to a state where the backend can never accept it.

docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1735.md:23232:  - [ ] Create `api/src/functions/complaints/partner-create.ts` â€” route `POST /v1/complaints`; auth: raw `verifyFirebaseIdToken` (handles both customer and technician tokens); fetch booking from Cosmos to derive `filedBy`; validate `reasonCode` against per-role enums; build `ComplaintDoc` with `filedBy`, `reasonCode`, `photoStoragePath`, `acknowledgeDeadlineAt = now+2h`, `slaDeadlineAt = now+24h`, `type = "STANDARD"`; duplicate-check query against complaints container; call `createComplaint()`; fire FCM `OWNER_COMPLAINT_FILED` to `owner_alerts` fire-and-forget; return 201
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1735.md:23235:- [ ] **T3 â€” API: `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}` endpoint (api/, TDD)**

docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1735.md:23248:  - [ ] Create `customer-app/.../data/complaint/remote/dto/ComplaintDtos.kt` â€” Moshi `@JsonClass(generateAdapter=true)` data classes: `CreateComplaintRequestDto`, `ComplaintResponseDto`, `ComplaintListResponseDto`
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1735.md:23311:`POST /v1/complaints` cannot use `requireCustomer` HOF because it must accept both customer and technician tokens. Pattern: call `verifyFirebaseIdToken(token)` directly, fetch the booking doc, check `uid == booking.customerId` â†’ `filedBy = CUSTOMER` or `uid == booking.technicianId` â†’ `filedBy = TECHNICIAN`; else 403. The server sets `filedBy` â€” the request body never includes it (prevents forgery).

docs\stories\E07-S03-complaints-module.md:91:  - [ ] Create `api/src/functions/complaints/partner-create.ts` — route `POST /v1/complaints`; auth: raw `verifyFirebaseIdToken` (handles both customer and technician tokens); fetch booking from Cosmos to derive `filedBy`; validate `reasonCode` against per-role enums; build `ComplaintDoc` with `filedBy`, `reasonCode`, `photoStoragePath`, `acknowledgeDeadlineAt = now+2h`, `slaDeadlineAt = now+24h`, `type = "STANDARD"`; duplicate-check query against complaints container; call `createComplaint()`; fire FCM `OWNER_COMPLAINT_FILED` to `owner_alerts` fire-and-forget; return 201
docs\stories\E07-S03-complaints-module.md:94:- [ ] **T3 — API: `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}` endpoint (api/, TDD)**
docs\stories\E07-S03-complaints-module.md:96:  - [ ] Create `api/src/functions/complaints/partner-get.ts` — route `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}`; same dual-role auth; return complaints for that booking filed by caller (`filedBy` + `uid` filter on query)
docs\stories\E07-S03-complaints-module.md:107:  - [ ] Create `customer-app/.../data/complaint/remote/dto/ComplaintDtos.kt` — Moshi `@JsonClass(generateAdapter=true)` data classes: `CreateComplaintRequestDto`, `ComplaintResponseDto`, `ComplaintListResponseDto`
docs\stories\E07-S03-complaints-module.md:170:`POST /v1/complaints` cannot use `requireCustomer` HOF because it must accept both customer and technician tokens. Pattern: call `verifyFirebaseIdToken(token)` directly, fetch the booking doc, check `uid == booking.customerId` → `filedBy = CUSTOMER` or `uid == booking.technicianId` → `filedBy = TECHNICIAN`; else 403. The server sets `filedBy` — the request body never includes it (prevents forgery).

docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1812-round4.md:23457:docs\stories\E07-S03-complaints-module.md:94:- [ ] **T3 — API: `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}` endpoint (api/, TDD)**
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1812-round4.md:23458:docs\stories\E07-S03-complaints-module.md:96:  - [ ] Create `api/src/functions/complaints/partner-get.ts` — route `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}`; same dual-role auth; return complaints for that booking filed by caller (`filedBy` + `uid` filter on query)
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1812-round4.md:23459:docs\stories\E07-S03-complaints-module.md:170:`POST /v1/complaints` cannot use `requireCustomer` HOF because it must accept both customer and technician tokens. Pattern: call `verifyFirebaseIdToken(token)` directly, fetch the booking doc, check `uid == booking.customerId` → `filedBy = CUSTOMER` or `uid == booking.technicianId` → `filedBy = TECHNICIAN`; else 403. The server sets `filedBy` — the request body never includes it (prevents forgery).

docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1812-round4.md:24110:  `loadStatus()` and `onSubmit()` both collapse the server response down to `complaintId` and `acknowledgeDeadlineAt`, so the confirmation screen has no way to show whether the latest complaint is `NEW`, `INVESTIGATING`, or `RESOLVED`. If the customer revisits a complaint after the owner updates it, the UI will still show the static “owner will respond in 2 hours” message instead of the current status returned by `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}`.
docs\reviews\codex-20260425-1812-round4.md:24128:  `loadStatus()` and `onSubmit()` both collapse the server response down to `complaintId` and `acknowledgeDeadlineAt`, so the confirmation screen has no way to show whether the latest complaint is `NEW`, `INVESTIGATING`, or `RESOLVED`. If the customer revisits a complaint after the owner updates it, the UI will still show the static “owner will respond in 2 hours” message instead of the current status returned by `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}`.

1:package com.homeservices.technician.ui.complaint
3:import android.net.Uri
4:import androidx.activity.compose.rememberLauncherForActivityResult
5:import androidx.activity.result.contract.ActivityResultContracts
6:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Arrangement
7:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Column
8:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Spacer
9:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize
10:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxWidth
11:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
12:import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.padding
13:import androidx.compose.material3.Button
14:import androidx.compose.material3.CircularProgressIndicator
15:import androidx.compose.material3.DropdownMenuItem
16:import androidx.compose.material3.ExperimentalMaterial3Api

24:import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable

27:import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableStateOf
28:import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
29:import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
30:import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
31:import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
32:import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
33:import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
34:import androidx.hilt.navigation.compose.hiltViewModel
35:import androidx.lifecycle.compose.collectAsStateWithLifecycle
36:import com.homeservices.technician.domain.complaint.TechComplaintReason
38:@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class)
39:@Composable
40:public fun ComplaintScreen(
41:    bookingId: String,
42:    onBack: () -> Unit,
43:    viewModel: ComplaintViewModel = hiltViewModel(),
44:) {
45:    val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
47:    LaunchedEffect(bookingId) { viewModel.loadStatus(bookingId) }
49:    when (val state = uiState) {
50:        is ComplaintUiState.Success -> {
51:            Column(
52:                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize().padding(24.dp),
53:                horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally,
54:                verticalArrangement = Arrangement.Center,
55:            ) {
56:                Text(text = "समस्या दर्ज हो गई।", style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineSmall)
57:                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
58:                Text(text = "मालिक 2 घंटे में जवाब देंगे।", style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium)
59:                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(24.dp))
60:                Button(onClick = onBack) { Text(text = "वापस जाएं") }
61:            }
62:        }
63:        is ComplaintUiState.PhotoUploading, ComplaintUiState.Submitting -> {
64:            Column(
65:                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
66:                horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally,
67:                verticalArrangement = Arrangement.Center,
68:            ) { CircularProgressIndicator() }
69:        }
70:        is ComplaintUiState.Error -> {
71:            Column(
72:                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize().padding(24.dp),
73:                verticalArrangement = Arrangement.Center,
74:            ) {
75:                Text(text = "त्रुटि: ${state.message}", color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error)
76:                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(16.dp))
77:                Button(onClick = { viewModel.onRetry() }) {
78:                    Text(text = "पुनः प्रयास करें")
79:                }
80:            }
81:        }
82:        is ComplaintUiState.Idle -> {
83:            var expanded by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
84:            val context = LocalContext.current
85:            val photoPicker =
86:                rememberLauncherForActivityResult(ActivityResultContracts.GetContent()) { uri: Uri? ->
87:                    uri ?: return@rememberLauncherForActivityResult
88:                    val tmpFile = java.io.File(context.cacheDir, "complaint_photo_${System.currentTimeMillis()}.jpg")
89:                    context.contentResolver.openInputStream(uri)?.use { input ->
90:                        tmpFile.outputStream().use { output -> input.copyTo(output) }
91:                    }
92:                    viewModel.onPhotoSelected(tmpFile.absolutePath, bookingId)
93:                }
94:            Column(
95:                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize().padding(16.dp),
96:                verticalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(16.dp),
97:            ) {
98:                Text(text = "समस्या रिपोर्ट करें", style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineSmall)
99:                ExposedDropdownMenuBox(expanded = expanded, onExpandedChange = { expanded = it }) {
100:                    OutlinedTextField(
101:                        value = state.selectedReason?.labelHindi ?: "कारण चुनें",
102:                        onValueChange = {},
103:                        readOnly = true,
104:                        label = { Text(text = "समस्या का कारण") },
105:                        trailingIcon = { ExposedDropdownMenuDefaults.TrailingIcon(expanded = expanded) },
106:                        modifier = Modifier.menuAnchor(MenuAnchorType.PrimaryNotEditable, true).fillMaxWidth(),
107:                    )
108:                    ExposedDropdownMenu(expanded = expanded, onDismissRequest = { expanded = false }) {
109:                        TechComplaintReason.entries.forEach { reason ->
110:                            DropdownMenuItem(
111:                                text = { Text(text = reason.labelHindi) },
112:                                onClick = {
113:                                    viewModel.onReasonSelected(reason)
114:                                    expanded = false
115:                                },
116:                            )
117:                        }
118:                    }
119:                }
120:                OutlinedTextField(
121:                    value = state.description,
122:                    onValueChange = { viewModel.onDescriptionChanged(it) },
123:                    label = { Text(text = "विवरण (10–2000 अक्षर)") },
124:                    supportingText = { Text(text = "${state.description.length}/2000") },
125:                    minLines = 4,
126:                    maxLines = 8,
127:                    modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
128:                )
129:                val photoButtonLabel =
130:                    if (state.photoStoragePath != null) "फ़ोटो बदलें ✓" else "फ़ोटो जोड़ें (वैकल्पिक)"
131:                OutlinedButton(
132:                    onClick = { photoPicker.launch("image/*") },
133:                    modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
134:                ) {
135:                    Text(text = photoButtonLabel)
136:                }
137:                Button(
138:                    onClick = { viewModel.onSubmit(bookingId) },
139:                    enabled = state.submitEnabled,
140:                    modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
141:                ) { Text(text = "समस्या दर्ज करें") }
142:            }
143:        }
144:    }
145:}

codex
The patch introduces at least one environment-breaking client configuration issue and a backend race that allows duplicate active complaints. It also misrepresents complaint state in the new UI after the complaint progresses beyond `NEW`.

Full review comments:

- [P1] Use the configured API base URL for technician complaints — C:\Alok\Business Projects\Urbanclap-dup\.worktrees\feature-E07-S03-complaints-module\technician-app\app\src\main\kotlin\com\homeservices\technician\data\complaint\di\ComplaintModule.kt:32-35
  This Retrofit module hard-codes the production Azure endpoint instead of using the app's environment-specific base URL like the other network modules. In debug/staging builds, complaint create/get calls will go to a different backend than the rest of the app, which breaks local testing and can also send real complaint traffic to production unexpectedly.

- [P2] Prevent concurrent duplicate complaint creation — C:\Alok\Business Projects\Urbanclap-dup\.worktrees\feature-E07-S03-complaints-module\api\src\functions\complaints\partner-create.ts:59-82
  The duplicate check and `createComplaint()` call are not atomic. If the same customer/technician submits twice in quick succession (for example, a double tap or client retry before the first response arrives), both requests can observe `existing == null` and each insert a new active complaint because the document ID is always a fresh UUID. That leaves multiple open complaints for the same booking/party pair despite the endpoint intending to reject duplicates.

- [P2] Show the actual complaint status instead of a fixed ack message — C:\Alok\Business Projects\Urbanclap-dup\.worktrees\feature-E07-S03-complaints-module\customer-app\app\src\main\kotlin\com\homeservices\customer\ui\complaint\ComplaintScreen.kt:59-66
  `loadStatus()` already passes through the server's complaint status, but the success UI ignores it and always says the owner will respond within 2 hours. When a user reopens a complaint that has already moved to `INVESTIGATING` or `RESOLVED`, this screen still presents it as a newly filed complaint, which is misleading and hides the current state returned by `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}`.
The patch introduces at least one environment-breaking client configuration issue and a backend race that allows duplicate active complaints. It also misrepresents complaint state in the new UI after the complaint progresses beyond `NEW`.

Full review comments:

- [P1] Use the configured API base URL for technician complaints — C:\Alok\Business Projects\Urbanclap-dup\.worktrees\feature-E07-S03-complaints-module\technician-app\app\src\main\kotlin\com\homeservices\technician\data\complaint\di\ComplaintModule.kt:32-35
  This Retrofit module hard-codes the production Azure endpoint instead of using the app's environment-specific base URL like the other network modules. In debug/staging builds, complaint create/get calls will go to a different backend than the rest of the app, which breaks local testing and can also send real complaint traffic to production unexpectedly.

- [P2] Prevent concurrent duplicate complaint creation — C:\Alok\Business Projects\Urbanclap-dup\.worktrees\feature-E07-S03-complaints-module\api\src\functions\complaints\partner-create.ts:59-82
  The duplicate check and `createComplaint()` call are not atomic. If the same customer/technician submits twice in quick succession (for example, a double tap or client retry before the first response arrives), both requests can observe `existing == null` and each insert a new active complaint because the document ID is always a fresh UUID. That leaves multiple open complaints for the same booking/party pair despite the endpoint intending to reject duplicates.

- [P2] Show the actual complaint status instead of a fixed ack message — C:\Alok\Business Projects\Urbanclap-dup\.worktrees\feature-E07-S03-complaints-module\customer-app\app\src\main\kotlin\com\homeservices\customer\ui\complaint\ComplaintScreen.kt:59-66
  `loadStatus()` already passes through the server's complaint status, but the success UI ignores it and always says the owner will respond within 2 hours. When a user reopens a complaint that has already moved to `INVESTIGATING` or `RESOLVED`, this screen still presents it as a newly filed complaint, which is misleading and hides the current state returned by `GET /v1/complaints/{bookingId}`.
